# Toggle

Controls one independent two-state interface mode.

## Installation

Add the Toggle component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/toggle
```

## Usage

Use Toggle for a stateful button such as bold formatting, showing a panel, or pinning a view. The pressed appearance and semantics describe whether that mode is on. Bind `v-model` to a boolean when the surrounding interface needs to read or change the state.

**Toggle example**

```vue
<template>
  <Toggle v-model="enabled" variant="outline">
    <BoldIcon />
    Bold
  </Toggle>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { BoldIcon } from '@lucide/vue'
import { ref } from 'vue'
import { Toggle } from '@/Components/Toggle'

const enabled = ref(false)
</script>
```

## Variants and sizes

Use the default variant in a toolbar where the surrounding context already makes controls visible. Use `outline` when the boundary needs more definition. Sizes are `sm`, `default`, and `lg`; keep adjacent controls at one size and reserve compact sizes for dense toolbars.

```vue
<Toggle variant="default" size="sm">Compact</Toggle>
<Toggle variant="outline" size="default">Outlined</Toggle>
```

## Controlled and disabled state

Use `default-value` for an initial uncontrolled pressed state or `v-model` for controlled state, not both. When a parent changes the model, the visual pressed state follows it. Disable a toggle only when the mode is unavailable, and explain why if that is not apparent from nearby content.

## Labels and icons

Prefer visible text when the action could be ambiguous. An icon-only Toggle needs an `aria-label` that names the mode, such as “Toggle bold,” while the pressed state is conveyed separately. Do not change the accessible name between its on and off states.

```vue
<Toggle v-model="isBold" aria-label="Toggle bold">
  <BoldIcon />
</Toggle>
```

## Status versus action

Toggle is an action with persistent pressed state, not a status label. Use Badge to display state without changing it and Button for a command that should run each time it is activated. Do not use Toggle for a navigation link or a destructive confirmation.

> **Use Switch for an immediate setting: [Switch](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/switch)**
>
> Use Switch for a binary application setting whose on/off state should be explicit. Use Toggle for a compact mode or tool within the current interface.

> **Use Toggle Group for related modes: [Toggle Group](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/toggle-group)**
>
> When several related toggles form one single- or multiple-selection control, use Toggle Group so their value and keyboard behavior are coordinated.

Hosted documentation: [Toggle](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/toggle)
